name: ClickUp API
what_it_does: ClickUp's REST API for managing tasks, spaces, lists, goals, time tracking, and documents in ClickUp's all-in-one productivity platform.
use_cases: Creating and updating tasks in ClickUp from agent workflows
Reading task status and progress for automated reporting
Managing time tracking entries for billing automation
Syncing project data between ClickUp and external systems
Automating task assignment based on agent-determined workload
not_for: Simple task tracking without ClickUp's complexity
High-frequency API calls (rate limits are restrictive)
best_when: An agent needs to interact with ClickUp's rich task and project management data, especially for automation workflows already centered on ClickUp.
avoid_when: You're not using ClickUp or need high-frequency API access.

auth.notes: Personal API tokens for single-user access. OAuth2 for multi-user applications. Personal tokens are the easiest path for agents. Tokens give full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited tasks, 100MB storage, limited views
pricing.notes: API access on all plans. Free plan is quite capable. Business plan needed for advanced automations and dashboards.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include err string and ECODE. Error messages are generally readable but error codes are not well-documented. Debugging requires trial and error.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Task creation creates duplicates on retry. No built-in deduplication mechanism.
known_agent_gotchas: 100 req/min rate limit is low for agents processing many tasks
Hierarchical data model (workspace > space > folder > list > task) requires understanding before querying
Custom fields require knowing their IDs which change per workspace
API v2 is current but documentation quality lags behind product changes
Subtask handling differs from parent task — requires flags in list requests
No idempotency — retrying task creation creates duplicate tasks
